item_spec.concrete_change:
Surface a fan-out-size signal from the dispatch backend and have the loader gate on it (the backend cannot force a human pause; in the conversation-first design it emits the signal and the host-facing prompt instructs the host to confirm). Changes:

(1) packages/audit-code/src/cli/dispatch.ts (prepareDispatchArtifacts): compute and surface the fan-out scale. agent_count = number of packets to be dispatched this run (after canary/budget from FINDING-008/009); wave_size comes from waveSchedule (FINDING-002); wave_count = Math.ceil(agent_count / Math.max(1, wave_size)). Add these plus `confirmation_recommended: boolean` and a human-readable `dispatch_summary` (e.g. 'This will launch <agent_count> agents across <wave_count> wave(s).') to the PrepareDispatchResult and to the persisted dispatch metadata (dispatch-quota.json or ActiveDispatchState). Set confirmation_recommended = agent_count > threshold, where threshold = sessionConfig.dispatch?.confirm_threshold (default a conservative value, e.g. 10). Keep it side-effect-free — no prompting in the backend.

(2) DISCOVERY TARGET — /audit-code loader / host-asset SOURCE template (rendered by `ensure`; locate in implement phase, edit the source not generated output): in the dispatch step guidance, instruct the host that when the dispatch step reports confirmation_recommended (or agent_count exceeds the threshold), it MUST pause and ask the user to confirm ('This will launch N agents across M waves — continue?') before launching the fan-out, and proceed only on confirmation. This addresses meta-audit-log O10 (106 agents launched with no human-in-the-loop checkpoint).

Composition: this is independent of but complementary to the canary (FINDING-008, de-risks correctness of one submit) and the budget (FINDING-009, caps total cost) — the gate is about informed consent to the remaining spend. CROSS-REFERENCE: add dispatch.confirm_threshold to SessionConfig (shared/src/types/sessionConfig.ts; coordinate with FINDING-005/008/009).

tests_to_write.name: dispatch surfaces an accurate fan-out summary and confirmation signal
assertions: When agent_count exceeds the configured confirm_threshold, prepareDispatchArtifacts sets confirmation_recommended = true and a dispatch_summary naming the agent_count and wave_count.
When agent_count is at or below the threshold, confirmation_recommended = false.
wave_count equals Math.ceil(agent_count / wave_size) for representative wave_size values (including wave_size 1).

tests_to_write.name: confirmation threshold is configurable with a safe default
assertions: With no threshold configured, the conservative default applies (small fan-outs do not trigger confirmation; large ones do).
